name: Rhubarb Tarts with Walnut Pastry
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Well-chilled unsalted butter (cut into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
PASTRY
1. In a medium bowl, combine flour, ground walnuts, and sugar.
2. Cut in the chilled butter until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Blend in ice water just until the dough holds together.
4. Wrap the dough in plastic and refrigerate thoroughly.
FOR PASTRY:
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
6. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
7. Cut out two 4 1/2-inch circles. Fit them into two 3 1/2-inch round tart pans.
8. Trim the edges of the dough even with the edge of the pan.
9. Prick the bottom of each tart shell with a fork.
10. Bake until crisp and lightly browned, about 25 minutes.
11. Let cool.
FOR FILLING:
12. In a medium saucepan over low heat, combine 1/3 cup water and 1/4 cup sugar.
13. Add rhubarb, cover, and bring to simmer, stirring gently once or twice.
14. Cook just until the rhubarb is tender, about 3 to 5 minutes.
15. Drain, reserving liquid. Taste and adjust sweetness if desired, adding more sugar if needed.
16. Return the reserved liquid to the saucepan.
17. Mix 2 tablespoons water with arrowroot in a small bowl.
18. Blend the mixture into the rhubarb cooking liquid over low heat and stir gently until thickened and clear, about 5 minutes.
19. Add the rhubarb to the saucepan and let cool.
20. Spoon the filling into the tart shells.
21. Melt the red currant jelly over very low heat.
22. Spoon or brush evenly over the filling.
23. Refrigerate the tarts until ready to serve.
24. Top each with a dollop of crème fraîche or sour cream.
Makes 2 servings.
